prompt: Play this short three-step World 8 ICM counterweight chain.

steps.prompt: Flop: Covered tournament pressure and survival cost should keep this line closed. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This covered-pressure flop step should fold.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. When the survival cost is already high and you are covered, this flop should fold rather than continue on a thin edge.
steps.why_v1: When the learner is the one carrying the survival cost, tournament pressure can make disciplined release better than a thin continue.

steps.prompt: Turn: Balanced pressure and a live stack still support controlled continuation. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This balanced-pressure turn step prefers call.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. With pressure still meaningful but not yet decisive, this turn should stay balanced and continue with a call.
steps.why_v1: When tournament pressure remains meaningful but not decisive, keeping the line controlled preserves flexibility better than forcing aggression.

steps.prompt: River: Covering leverage and cleaner ICM pressure now justify a stronger close. Choose action.
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. This leverage-clean river step supports raise.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. Once the line closes cleanly and you still hold the covering leverage, this river should convert that edge into a raise.
steps.why_v1: When the learner is applying pressure while covering the opponent and the line closes cleanly, tournament leverage can justify the stronger finish.
